title: platform-user — field classification
description: PlatformUser + PlatformProfile carry two field classes: EMPLOYMENT metadata (hire_date, department, employment_type, job_title, manager_id) which is `internal` — visible to Academorix ops but never leaks to tenants — and PII (phone, avatar, Slack/GitHub handles) on PlatformProfile which is `restricted` — gated behind the platform-admin `platform.platform_profiles.view.pii` permission. Credential fields live on the referenced Identity row and are governed by identity::data-classes (regulated_secret). This module classifies its OWN columns only. The audit trail (via HasAudit) writes every column mutation — old + new values — because SOC2 CC7.2 requires the fuller trail on the staff plane.

confidential: PlatformUser.offboarding_reason (free-form justification captured at offboard time; contains HR-sensitive context)
PlatformUser.on_call_rotation_key (opaque identifier from the external on-call provider e.g. PagerDuty schedule id — not a secret but names an internal escalation path)

wire_projection.description: PlatformUser + PlatformProfile are `platform_admin`-guard-only surfaces. There is NO tenant-facing wire shape — the row is never returned on a `sanctum`-guarded response. Two platform wire shapes: the LIST view (redacted, no PII, no offboarding_reason) and the DETAIL view (may unmute PII when the caller holds the corresponding permission).
wire_projection.tenant_wire_shape: N/A — no tenant endpoint returns PlatformUser or PlatformProfile fields directly. Impersonation surfaces (Wave 1b) expose only the impersonator's display_name via a derived DTO.

platform_admin_list_shape.display_name: PlatformProfile.display_name OR concat(first_name, ' ', last_name) if display_name is null
platform_admin_list_shape.mfa_enabled: boolean derived from Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted IS NOT NULL
platform_admin_list_shape.reports_to: PlatformUser.manager_id resolved to a compact reference { id, display_name }
platform_admin_list_shape.avatar_url: PlatformProfile.avatar_url (REDACTED to null unless caller has `platform.platform_profiles.view.pii`)

platform_admin_detail_shape.manager: resolved to compact reference { id, display_name, department } OR null

on_call.current_shift: resolved via OnCallResolver — null if not in rotation

never_returned: PlatformUser.identity_id (implementation detail — exposed only through the derived `email` + `mfa_enabled` accessors)
PlatformUser.profile_id (implementation detail — satellite loaded via nested `profile` object)

shape.identity_id: ULID (validated: identity row exists, is not soft-deleted, has email_verified, has mfa_enabled)
shape.display_name: string | null (optional; if null, computed from first + last)
shape.phone_e164: string (validated: valid_e164_phone; on-call rotations require reachable phone)
shape.hire_date: date (required, cannot be in the future)
shape.manager_id: ULID | null (validated: manager_belongs_to_platform — must reference an active PlatformUser, cannot be self)

safety_gates: Rejects if Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted IS NULL — platform_admin guard requires MFA enrolment BEFORE hire (config('platform-user.mfa.require_on_hire')).
Rejects if another PlatformUser row already references the same identity_id (UNIQUE constraint).

input_dtos.scope: Only the PlatformUser themselves OR a super_admin can invoke.

shape.reason: string (required, min 20 chars, max 1000 — becomes offboarding_reason on the row)
shape.effective_at: timestamp | null (default = now(); allow scheduling up to 30 days out for planned offboardings)

safety_gates: Actor must hold `platform.platform_users.offboard` permission (super_admin by default).
If manager_id points at other PlatformUsers who report to this row, refuses with `MANAGER_HAS_REPORTS` — requires reassignment first.

shape.new_manager_id: ULID | null (null = orphans the report to top-of-tree)

output_dtos.purpose: Standalone profile shape for the `/platform-profiles/me` self-service endpoint. Always unredacted for the caller (they own the row) — gated only by the standard `sanctum` self-view rules on the platform_admin guard.

current_shift.phone_e164: string (unredacted — on-call escalation requires reachable phone; caller has `platform.on_call.view` permission)

shape.next_shift: same shape OR null when no upcoming shift is registered

output_dtos.purpose: Response body of `GET /platform-users/on-call/current`. Consumed by internal escalation tooling.

shape.recent_events: array (paginated) of { at, event, target_type, target_id, correlation_id }

output_dtos.purpose: Response body of `GET /platform-users/{platformUser}/audit-trail`. Aggregates audit + activity rows keyed by this PlatformUser for a rolling 90-day window.

notes: Redactor rules apply to every restricted-tier field by default. The redactor consults the request context to unmute when the caller holds the corresponding `platform.platform_profiles.view.pii` or `platform.platform_users.view.pii` permission.

SOC2 CC7.2 requires the fuller audit trail on the staff plane — HasAudit records EVERY column mutation, including internal-tier fields (department, job_title, manager_id) where the tenant plane would skip audit for the same tier.

The 90-day retention hold post-offboard preserves the audit-actor resolution: audit rows written before offboard still resolve to the display_name during that window. After hard delete, audit rows resolve to an anonymised placeholder (`[Former Platform Staff]`).

PlatformUser.metadata is an opaque JSON dictionary the platform-ops team uses for tooling hints (e.g. `hr_system_id`, `laptop_asset_tag`, `office_location`). Never stores tenant or user data — internal-tier only.
The `on_call_rotation_key` column is set only when the PlatformUser is registered in an external rotation (PagerDuty schedule id). Non-null triggers the on-call-lookup path in OnCallResolver.
